data.body:
## Description

Two bugs in record-test-status.sh prevent RED markers from being applied when a shared test file is triggered via a non-marked .test-index association.

**Bug A (marker overwrite)**: Line 365 unconditionally overwrites the marker map. When multiple staged source files map to the same test, the last-processed entry's marker wins — an empty marker can erase a previously set marker.

**Bug B (marker only consulted for staged sources)**: The script only reads .test-index entries for staged source files. If the source with the RED marker is not staged, but a different staged source maps to the same test without a marker, the RED marker is invisible.

## Root Cause
record-test-status.sh treats RED markers as properties of source→test associations rather than properties of test files. When the test is reachable via multiple association paths, only the path actually traversed determines whether the marker applies.

## Fix
Bug A: Conditional assignment at line 365 (non-empty marker wins).
Bug B: Global marker scan after deduplication — parse .test-index properly (not substring grep) to find markers for any test file that lacks one from the staged-source path.

Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Single file, clear root cause.
